---
title: Node Classification
subtitle: classifying network nodes by type and connectivity
description: A short description
author:
 - name: Pukar Bhandari
   email: pukar.bhandari@wfrc.utah.gov
   affiliation:
     - name: Wasatch Front Regional Council
       url: "https://wfrc.utah.gov/"
date: "2026-03-13"
---

## Setup Environment

### Import Libraries

```{python}
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

import gtfs_kit as gk

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd()))
from _src.node_utils import nodes_on, count_links, assign_directions, snap_nodes, snap_transit
```

### Filepath Configuration

```{python}
config_filepath = {
    # Input files
    "in_centerlines":      Path("_data/raw/BangerterEdits.gdb.zip"),
    "in_network_links":    Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Link.gpkg"),
    "in_network_nodes":    Path("_data/raw/WFv1000_MasterNet - Node.gpkg"),
    "in_gtfs":             Path("_data/raw/uta_gtfs.zip"),
    # Output files
    "out_node_classified": Path("_data/processed/nodes_classified.gpkg"),
    "out_node_snapped":    Path("_data/processed/nodes_snapped.gpkg"),
}
```

```{python}
CRS_UTM = "EPSG:26912"   # NAD83 UTM Zone 12N — Utah (metric)
CRS_WGS = "EPSG:4326"    # WGS84 — required by Folium
```

## Load Data

### Roadway Centerlines

```{python}
county_list = ["49003", "49057", "49011", "49035", "49049"]
target_fips = "(" + ", ".join([f"'{f}'" for f in county_list]) + ")"

gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file(
    f"/vsizip/{config_filepath['in_centerlines']}",
    layer="RoadsBangerterEtc",
    where=f"COUNTY_L IN {target_fips} OR COUNTY_R IN {target_fips}",
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

# Strip leading/trailing whitespace from string columns (source data artefact)
for col in ["DOT_FCLASS", "CARTOCODE", "STATUS", "ONEWAY", "VERT_LEVEL", "POSTTYPE"]:
    if col in gdf_centerlines.columns:
        gdf_centerlines[col] = gdf_centerlines[col].str.strip()

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_centerlines.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Links

Only links present in 2023 (LN_2023 > 0) are loaded. Links with LN_2023 == 0
are future links that do not yet exist as physical roadways and have no
corresponding centerline geometry.

```{python}
gdf_links = gpd.read_file(config_filepath["in_network_links"], where="LN_2023 > 0").to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_links):,} present-year links (LN_2023 > 0) | CRS: {gdf_links.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_links.head(1000).explore()
```

### Network Nodes

```{python}
gdf_nodes = gpd.read_file(config_filepath["in_network_nodes"]).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Loaded {len(gdf_nodes):,} features | CRS: {gdf_nodes.crs}")
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

gdf_nodes.head(1000).explore()
```

### UTA GTFS Feed

```{python}
feed = gk.read_feed(config_filepath["in_gtfs"], dist_units="km")

gdf_stops  = gk.get_stops(feed,  as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_shapes = gk.get_shapes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_routes = gk.get_routes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

print(f"Stops: {len(gdf_stops):,} | Shapes: {len(gdf_shapes):,} | Routes: {len(gdf_routes):,}")
```


### Rail Stops

Filter GTFS stops to Light Rail (route_type 0) and Commuter Rail (route_type 2) only,
walking the routes → trips → stop_times → stops chain.

```{python}
# GTFS route_type: 0 = Light Rail (TRAX), 2 = Commuter Rail (FrontRunner), 3 = Bus
RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES = [0, 2]

rail_route_ids = feed.routes.loc[feed.routes["route_type"].isin(RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES), "route_id"]
rail_trip_ids  = feed.trips.loc[feed.trips["route_id"].isin(rail_route_ids), "trip_id"]
rail_stop_ids  = feed.stop_times.loc[
    feed.stop_times["trip_id"].isin(rail_trip_ids), "stop_id"
].unique()

gdf_rail_stops = gdf_stops[gdf_stops["stop_id"].isin(rail_stop_ids)].copy()
print(f"Rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops):,} of {len(gdf_stops):,} total stops")
```

```{python}
# | eval: false

gdf_rail_stops.explore()
```

## Node Classification

### Functional Type

Each flag column is assigned using `nodes_on()` with a pandas query string, or plain
pandas expressions for N-range conditions. Combine with `&` (AND) or `|` (OR).
To add a column: add a line. To remove: delete it. To change logic: edit in place.

```{python}
# Freeway FT codes excl. CD (21, 31) and excl. HOV/Managed (37, 38, 39)
FW = list((set(range(20, 28)) | set(range(30, 37))) - {21, 31})

gdf_nodes["Freeway"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, f"FT_2023 in {FW}")
gdf_nodes["CD"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [21, 31]")
gdf_nodes["HOV"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [37, 38]")  # HOV and Managed lanes
gdf_nodes["ManagedAccess"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 == 39")
gdf_nodes["Expressway"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [12, 13, 14, 15]")
gdf_nodes["Arterial"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [2, 3]")
gdf_nodes["Collector"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [4, 5]")
gdf_nodes["Local"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [6, 7]")
gdf_nodes["Ramp"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [28, 29, 41, 42]")
gdf_nodes["CentroidConnector"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 == 1")
gdf_nodes["CentroidNode"] = gdf_nodes["N"] <= 3_600  # TAZ N: 1–3600
gdf_nodes["ExternalNode"] = gdf_nodes["N"].between(3_601, 9_999)  # External: 3601–9999
gdf_nodes["ConnectorOnly"] = (
    gdf_nodes["CentroidConnector"] & ~gdf_nodes["CentroidNode"] & ~gdf_nodes["ExternalNode"]
)
gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] = (
    nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, "FT_2023 in [70, 80]")
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(10_000, 19_999)
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(50_000, 59_999)
)
```

```{python}
flag_cols = [
    "N", "Freeway", "CD", "Expressway", "Arterial", "Collector", "Local",
    "Ramp", "HOV", "FixedTransit", "CentroidConnector", "CentroidNode", "ExternalNode", "ConnectorOnly",
]
gdf_nodes[flag_cols].head(20)
```

#### Summary

```{python}
gdf_nodes[flag_cols[1:]].sum().rename("NodeCount").to_frame()
```

### Link Count

#### Count Links and Assign Directions per Node

```{python}
# Existing function
gdf_nodes = count_links(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)

# NEW function for Phase 1
gdf_nodes = assign_directions(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)

# View the results
gdf_nodes[["N", "LinkCount", "link_directions"]].head(20)
```

#### Distribution of Link Counts

```{python}
link_count_freq = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"].value_counts().sort_index()
max_val = link_count_freq.max()

BASE_COLOR    = "#B4B2A9"   # c-gray 200  — default
ACCENT_COLOR  = "#1D9E75"   # c-teal 400  — typical intersection nodes (4, 6, 8)
PSEUDO_COLOR  = "#EF9F27"   # c-amber 400 — pseudonodes (2 links)
LABEL_COLOR   = "#444441"   # c-gray 800
SUBTITLE_COLOR= "#888780"   # c-gray 400
GRID_COLOR    = "#D3D1C7"   # c-gray 100

ACCENT_COUNTS = {4, 6, 8}
PSEUDO_COUNTS = {2}


def bar_color(link_count):
    if link_count in PSEUDO_COUNTS:
        return PSEUDO_COLOR
    if link_count in ACCENT_COUNTS:
        return ACCENT_COLOR
    return BASE_COLOR


bar_colors = [bar_color(i) for i in link_count_freq.index]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5.5))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")

ax.yaxis.grid(True, color=GRID_COLOR, linewidth=0.6, zorder=0)
ax.set_axisbelow(True)

bars = ax.bar(
    link_count_freq.index, link_count_freq.values,
    color=bar_colors, width=0.75, zorder=2,
)

for bar in bars:
    h = bar.get_height()
    ax.text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
        h + max_val * 0.012,
        f"{int(h):,}",
        ha="center", va="bottom",
        fontsize=9.5, color=LABEL_COLOR, fontweight="normal",
    )

for spine in ["top", "right", "left"]:
    ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(GRID_COLOR)

ax.set_xticks(link_count_freq.index)
ax.set_xticklabels(link_count_freq.index, fontsize=11, color=LABEL_COLOR)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}"))
ax.tick_params(axis="y", labelsize=9.5, colors=SUBTITLE_COLOR, length=0)
ax.tick_params(axis="x", length=0)
ax.set_ylim(0, max_val * 1.18)

ax.set_xlabel("Number of links connected to node", fontsize=10.5, color=SUBTITLE_COLOR, labelpad=10)
ax.set_ylabel("")

legend_elements = [
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=ACCENT_COLOR),
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=PSEUDO_COLOR),
    plt.Rectangle((0, 0), 1, 1, color=BASE_COLOR),
]
ax.legend(
    legend_elements,
    ["Typical intersection (4, 6, 8)", "Pseudonode (2)", "Other"],
    loc="upper right", fontsize=9, frameon=False,
    labelcolor=SUBTITLE_COLOR, handlelength=1, handleheight=0.8,
)

fig.text(
    0.13, 1.01,
    "Most nodes connect to 4, 6, or 8 links",
    fontsize=14, fontweight="bold", color=LABEL_COLOR,
    transform=ax.transAxes,
)
fig.text(
    0.13, 0.94,
    f"Distribution of {len(gdf_nodes):,} network nodes by link count",
    fontsize=10.5, color=SUBTITLE_COLOR,
    transform=ax.transAxes,
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Export

```{python}
out_path = config_filepath["out_node_classified"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes.to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Classified nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```

## Node Snapping

All data is in UTM (metres) so distance calculations are metric throughout.
The approach is piecemeal — snap one road type, validate it looks correct,
then move to the next. Do not proceed to the next section until the current
one looks right on the map.

### Prepare Centerlines

```{python}
import folium
from shapely.geometry import LineString, Point
from shapely.strtree import STRtree

# Exclude Retired and Planned rows
active_cl = gdf_centerlines[~gdf_centerlines["STATUS"].isin(["Retired", "Planned"])]
print(f"Active centerlines: {len(active_cl):,} of {len(gdf_centerlines):,}")
```

---

## Step 1 — Freeway Node Snapping

Freeway nodes (FT 20–40 excl. CD 21/31) snapped to Interstate and Other
Freeway centerline endpoints. HOV nodes (FT 37/38) and pseudonodes
(LinkCount == 2) are excluded — they will be handled separately.

### Step 1a — Build the Snap Mask

```{python}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Two binary flags describe every node's topological role.
# Their combination determines which centerline pool it snaps to.
#
#   is_fwy_node  — node touches at least one freeway link (FT 20-40 family).
#                  ManagedAccess-only nodes are excluded: they sit on HOT
#                  lanes with no corresponding mainline centerline endpoint.
#
#   is_ic_node   — node touches at least one Ramp or CD link.
#
# Flag combination → snap pass:
#
#   is_fwy_node=1, is_ic_node=0  →  Pass 1: mainline endpoints only
#   is_fwy_node=0, is_ic_node=1  →  Pass 2: interchange endpoints only
#   is_fwy_node=1, is_ic_node=1  →  Pass 3: mainline + interchange endpoints
#
# Pseudonodes (LinkCount == 2) are excluded from all passes — they are
# abstract shape vertices with no physical counterpart endpoint.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
no_pseudo   = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"] != 2
is_fwy_node = gdf_nodes["Freeway"] & ~gdf_nodes["ManagedAccess"] & no_pseudo
is_ic_node  = (gdf_nodes["Ramp"] | gdf_nodes["CD"]) & no_pseudo

print(f"is_fwy_node=1, is_ic_node=0  (Pass 1 — pure mainline):   {(is_fwy_node & ~is_ic_node).sum():,}")
print(f"is_fwy_node=0, is_ic_node=1  (Pass 2 — pure interchange): {(~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node).sum():,}")
print(f"is_fwy_node=1, is_ic_node=1  (Pass 3 — gore / mixed):     {(is_fwy_node & is_ic_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Floating (ManagedAccess-only, not snapped):                "
      f"{(gdf_nodes['Freeway'] & gdf_nodes['ManagedAccess'] & ~is_ic_node & no_pseudo).sum():,}")
```

### Step 1b — Pre-Snap Validation

Check distance distribution BEFORE snapping to confirm the centerline
coverage is adequate and the threshold is sensible.

```{python}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Centerline type filters — defined once, reused across ALL snap steps.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Mainline: Filter by authoritative UDOT Route Numbers (first 4 chars of DOT_RTNAME)
FREEWAY_ROUTES = ["0015", "0067", "0080", "0084", "0089", "0092", "0154", "0177", "0201", "0215"]
is_mainline = active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[:4].isin(FREEWAY_ROUTES)

# 0015: Interstate 15
# 0067: Legacy Parkway
# 0080: Interstate 80
# 0084: Interstate 84
# 0089: US-89
# 0092: Timpanogas Highway
# 0154: Bangerter Highway
# 0177: West Davis Corridor
# 0201: SR-201
# 0215: Interstate 215

# --- OLD APPROACH (Preserved for reference/fallback) ---
# is_mainline = active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Interstate", "Other Freeway"]) | (
#     active_cl["POSTTYPE"] == "FWY"
# )

# 2. Ramp: official UDOT Ramp (LRS 6th char 'R') OR locally tagged as RAMP.
is_ramp = (active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[5] == "R") | (active_cl["POSTTYPE"] == "RAMP")
# 3. CD (Collector-Distributor): LRS 6th char 'C'.
is_cd = active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].str[5] == "C"

# Ramp and CD are treated as a single "interchange" class throughout snapping.
# A model node with FT 31 (CD) regularly sits at a physical Ramp endpoint and
# vice versa — they are topologically interchangeable at gore points. Using a
# unified pool prevents false mismatches in both directions.
# Note: _assign_line_directions in node_utils.py clears allowed_dirs for all
# R and C segments so they are direction-agnostic during matching.
is_interchange = is_ramp | is_cd
```

```{python}
# For pre-validation, look at the full freeway corridor (mainline + interchange).
# All three pass populations are eligible, so union of both flags covers them.
fwy_cl = active_cl[is_mainline | is_interchange]
fwy_nodes_eligible = gdf_nodes[is_fwy_node | is_ic_node].copy()

print(f"Eligible freeway nodes:       {len(fwy_nodes_eligible):,}")
print(f"Freeway centerline segments:  {len(fwy_cl):,}")

# Distance from each eligible node to its nearest physical centerline segment
tree = STRtree(fwy_cl.geometry.values)
dists = np.array(
    [g.distance(fwy_cl.geometry.iloc[tree.nearest(g)]) for g in fwy_nodes_eligible.geometry]
)
fwy_nodes_eligible["nearest_line_m"] = np.round(dists, 1)

print(f"\nDistance to nearest physical pavement (m):")
print(f"  min:    {dists.min():.1f}")
print(f"  median: {np.median(dists):.1f}")
print(f"  p75:    {np.percentile(dists, 75):.1f}")
print(f"  p95:    {np.percentile(dists, 95):.1f}")
print(f"  max:    {dists.max():.1f}")
print(f"\n  ≤  50m: {(dists <= 50).sum():,}  ({(dists <= 50).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  ≤ 200m: {(dists <= 200).sum():,}  ({(dists <= 200).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  ≤ 500m: {(dists <= 500).sum():,}  ({(dists <= 500).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  > 500m: {(dists > 500).sum():,}  ({(dists > 500).mean() * 100:.0f}%)")

print(f"\n10 CLOSEST nodes:")
print(fwy_nodes_eligible.nsmallest(10, "nearest_line_m")[["N", "nearest_line_m"]].to_string())
```

### Step 1c — Pre-Snap Map

Blue = within 200m (good), Amber = 200–500m (borderline), Red = beyond 500m (no nearby centerline).

```{python}
# | eval: false

fwy_nodes_wgs = fwy_nodes_eligible.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
fwy_cl_wgs = fwy_cl.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

center = [fwy_nodes_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), fwy_nodes_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m_pre_fwy = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")

cl_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Freeway centerlines", show=True)
for _, row in fwy_cl_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.GeoJson(
        row.geometry.__geo_interface__,
        style_function=lambda _: {"color": "#1D9E75", "weight": 2, "opacity": 0.6},
    ).add_to(cl_layer)
cl_layer.add_to(m_pre_fwy)

node_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Freeway nodes (eligible)", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_nodes_wgs.iterrows():
    dist = fwy_nodes_eligible.loc[idx, "nearest_line_m"]
    color = "#E24B4A" if dist > 500 else "#EF9F27" if dist > 200 else "#378ADD"
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x],
        radius=4,
        color=color,
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.85,
        tooltip=f"N: {fwy_nodes_eligible.loc[idx, 'N']} | Line: {dist}m",
    ).add_to(node_layer)
node_layer.add_to(m_pre_fwy)

folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m_pre_fwy)
m_pre_fwy
```

### Step 1d — Snap Freeway and Interchange Nodes

Only run this once you are satisfied with Step 1b/1c.

```{python}
gdf_nodes_snapped = gdf_nodes.copy()

# is_mainline, is_interchange defined in Step 1b.
# is_fwy_node, is_ic_node defined in Step 1a.
# Passes are ordered so that the narrowest pools claim their endpoints first.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pass 1 — is_fwy_node=1, is_ic_node=0 → mainline endpoints only.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pure freeway nodes. Pool is kept narrow to prevent drift onto adjacent ramps.
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    active_cl[is_mainline],
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & ~is_ic_node,
    max_distance_m=250,
    label="Freeway_Mainline",
    target_id_cols=["DOT_RTNAME", "DOT_RTPART", "DOT_HWYNAM"],
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pass 2 — is_fwy_node=0, is_ic_node=1 → interchange endpoints only.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pure ramp/CD nodes. Must run before Pass 3 so gore-point interchange
# endpoints are not consumed by the wider mixed-node pool first.
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    active_cl[is_interchange],
    node_mask=~is_fwy_node & is_ic_node,
    max_distance_m=100,
    label="Interchange",
    target_id_cols=["DOT_RTNAME", "DOT_RTPART", "DOT_HWYNAM"],
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pass 3 — is_fwy_node=1, is_ic_node=1 → mainline + interchange endpoints.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Gore / mixed nodes. Widest pool, runs last. These nodes sit at the shared
# endpoint between a mainline and a ramp or CD segment and need access to both.
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    active_cl[is_mainline | is_interchange],
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & is_ic_node,
    max_distance_m=250,
    label="Freeway_Junction",
    target_id_cols=["DOT_RTNAME", "DOT_RTPART", "DOT_HWYNAM"],
)
```

### Step 1e — Post-Snap Validation Map

Compare original (red) vs snapped (green) positions.
Connector lines show how far each node moved.

```{python}
# | eval: false

# UPDATE: Filter for both Mainline and Junction labels
fwy_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(["Freeway_Mainline", "Freeway_Junction"])
]
fwy_original = gdf_nodes.loc[fwy_snapped.index]

fwy_snapped_wgs = fwy_snapped.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
fwy_original_wgs = fwy_original.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors = []
for idx in fwy_snapped.index:
    orig = fwy_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    snap = fwy_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    if orig.distance(snap) > 0:
        connectors.append(
            {
                "geometry": LineString([orig, snap]),
                "N": int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "N"]),
                "dist_m": float(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"]),
            }
        )
gdf_fwy_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors, crs=CRS_WGS)

center = [fwy_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), fwy_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m_post_fwy = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")

conn_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Displacement lines", show=True)
for _, row in gdf_fwy_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine(
        [(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
        color="#888780",
        weight=1,
        opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | moved {row['dist_m']}m",
    ).add_to(conn_layer)
conn_layer.add_to(m_post_fwy)

orig_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Original positions", show=True)
for idx, row in fwy_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x],
        radius=4,
        color="#E24B4A",
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.8,
        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)",
    ).add_to(orig_layer)
orig_layer.add_to(m_post_fwy)

snap_layer = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Snapped positions", show=True)
RULE_COLORS = {
    "Freeway_Mainline": "#1D9E75",  # teal
    "Interchange":      "#EF9F27",  # amber
    "Freeway_Junction": "#534AB7",  # purple
}
for idx, row in fwy_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    rule = fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"]
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x],
        radius=4,
        color=RULE_COLORS.get(rule, "#888780"),
        fill=True,
        fill_opacity=0.8,
        tooltip=f"N: {int(fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {rule} | {fwy_snapped.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m",
    ).add_to(snap_layer)
snap_layer.add_to(m_post_fwy)

folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m_post_fwy)
m_post_fwy
```

### Step 1f — Post-Snap Distance Summary

```{python}
ALL_SNAP_RULES = ["Freeway_Mainline", "Interchange", "Freeway_Junction"]

for rule in ALL_SNAP_RULES:
    dists = gdf_nodes_snapped.loc[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == rule, "snap_distance_m"]
    if len(dists) == 0:
        print(f"{rule}: 0 nodes snapped")
        continue
    print(f"{rule} ({len(dists):,} nodes):")
    print(f"  median: {dists.median():.1f}m  |  p90: {dists.quantile(0.9):.1f}m  |  max: {dists.max():.1f}m")
    print(f"  < 50m:  {(dists < 50).sum():,}  ({(dists < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
    print()

all_eligible = is_fwy_node | is_ic_node
exceeded = gdf_nodes_snapped[all_eligible & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")]
print(f"Nodes exceeded threshold (all passes): {len(exceeded):,}")
print(f"  Review in QGIS — abstract placement or missing centerline endpoint.")
```

### Step 1g — Export Snapped Nodes for QGIS

```{python}
ALL_SNAP_RULES = ["Freeway_Mainline", "Interchange", "Freeway_Junction"]
all_eligible   = is_fwy_node | is_ic_node

# 1. All successfully snapped nodes across all three passes
fwy_snapped  = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"].isin(ALL_SNAP_RULES)].copy()
fwy_original = gdf_nodes.loc[fwy_snapped.index].copy()

# 2. Displacement lines
lines = []
for idx in fwy_snapped.index:
    orig_geom = fwy_original.loc[idx, "geometry"]
    snap_geom = fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "geometry"]
    if orig_geom.distance(snap_geom) > 0:
        lines.append({
            "N": fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "N"],
            "snap_rule": fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_rule"],
            "snap_distance_m": fwy_snapped.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"],
            "geometry": LineString([orig_geom, snap_geom]),
        })

gdf_fwy_lines = (
    gpd.GeoDataFrame(lines, crs=CRS_UTM)
    if lines
    else gpd.GeoDataFrame(columns=["N", "snap_rule", "snap_distance_m", "geometry"], crs=CRS_UTM)
)

# 3. Nodes that exceeded threshold — for manual QGIS review
fwy_unsnapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    all_eligible & (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold")
].copy()

# 4. Export to a single GeoPackage
fwy_debug_out_path = Path("_data/processed/debug_freeway_snapping.gpkg")
fwy_debug_out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

fwy_snapped.to_file(fwy_debug_out_path,  layer="snapped_positions",   driver="GPKG")
fwy_original.to_file(fwy_debug_out_path, layer="original_positions",   driver="GPKG")
if not gdf_fwy_lines.empty:
    gdf_fwy_lines.to_file(fwy_debug_out_path, layer="displacement_lines", driver="GPKG")
if not fwy_unsnapped.empty:
    fwy_unsnapped.to_file(fwy_debug_out_path, layer="unsnapped_nodes",    driver="GPKG")

print(f"Debug package saved to: {fwy_debug_out_path}")
print(f"  snapped_positions: {len(fwy_snapped):,}")
print(f"  unsnapped_nodes:   {len(fwy_unsnapped):,}")
```

---

## Step 2 — Surface Street Node Snapping

Arterial, Collector, and Local nodes snapped as a group to State Highway
and local road centerlines (CARTOCODE 4, 5, 8, 10, 11). Split into
separate calls if per-type control is needed.

### Step 3a — Snap Surface Nodes

```{python}
surface_snap_mask = (
    (gdf_nodes_snapped["Arterial"] | gdf_nodes_snapped["Collector"] | gdf_nodes_snapped["Local"])
    & (gdf_nodes_snapped["LinkCount"] != 2)  # exclude pseudonodes
)
print(f"Surface nodes eligible for snapping: {surface_snap_mask.sum():,}")

gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    active_cl[active_cl["CARTOCODE"].isin(["4", "5", "8", "10", "11"])],
    node_mask=surface_snap_mask,
    max_distance_m=250,
    label="Surface",
    target_id_cols=["DOT_RTNAME", "DOT_RTPART", "DOT_HWYNAM"],
)
```

### Step 3b — Post-Snap Summary (Surface)

```{python}
surf_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface"]["snap_distance_m"]
print(f"Surface nodes snapped:   {len(surf_snapped):,}")
print(f"  median move:           {surf_snapped.median():.1f}m")
print(f"  max move:              {surf_snapped.max():.1f}m")
print(f"  moved < 50m:           {(surf_snapped < 50).sum():,}  ({(surf_snapped < 50).mean()*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  moved 50–200m:         {((surf_snapped >= 50) & (surf_snapped <= 200)).sum():,}")
```

### Step 3c — Surface Post-Snap Map

```{python}
#| eval: false

surf_snapped_gdf  = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "Surface"]
surf_original_gdf = gdf_nodes.loc[surf_snapped_gdf.index]

surf_snapped_wgs  = surf_snapped_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)
surf_original_wgs = surf_original_gdf.to_crs(CRS_WGS)

connectors_surf = []
for idx in surf_snapped_gdf.index:
    orig = surf_original_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    snap = surf_snapped_wgs.geometry.loc[idx]
    if orig.distance(snap) > 0:
        connectors_surf.append({
            "geometry": LineString([orig, snap]),
            "N": int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "N"]),
            "dist_m": float(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, "snap_distance_m"]),
        })
gdf_surf_connectors = gpd.GeoDataFrame(connectors_surf, crs=CRS_WGS)

center = [surf_original_wgs.geometry.y.mean(), surf_original_wgs.geometry.x.mean()]
m_post_surf = folium.Map(location=center, zoom_start=11, tiles="CartoDB positron")

for _, row in gdf_surf_connectors.iterrows():
    folium.PolyLine(
        [(c[1], c[0]) for c in row.geometry.coords],
        color="#888780", weight=1, opacity=0.5,
        tooltip=f"N: {row['N']} | {row['dist_m']}m",
    ).add_to(m_post_surf)

for idx, row in surf_original_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=3,
        color="#E24B4A", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {gdf_nodes.loc[idx, 'N']} (original)",
    ).add_to(m_post_surf)

for idx, row in surf_snapped_wgs.iterrows():
    folium.CircleMarker(
        location=[row.geometry.y, row.geometry.x], radius=3,
        color="#1D9E75", fill=True, fill_opacity=0.7,
        tooltip=f"N: {int(surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'N'])} | {surf_snapped_gdf.loc[idx, 'snap_distance_m']}m",
    ).add_to(m_post_surf)

folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False).add_to(m_post_surf)
m_post_surf
```

---

## Step 4 — Transit Node Snapping

FixedTransit nodes snapped to Light Rail (TRAX) and Commuter Rail
(FrontRunner) stops only — bus stops are excluded.

### Step 4a — Snap Transit Nodes

```{python}
# Group twin platforms by station name to create a single centralized anchor
rail_stops_grouped = []
for name, group in gdf_rail_stops.groupby("stop_name"):
    merged_geom = group.geometry.union_all().centroid
    merged_ids = ",".join(group["stop_id"].astype(str).unique())
    rail_stops_grouped.append({"stop_name": name, "stop_id": merged_ids, "geometry": merged_geom})

gdf_rail_stops_grouped = gpd.GeoDataFrame(rail_stops_grouped, crs=CRS_UTM)

# Snap using the combined station centroids
gdf_nodes_snapped = snap_transit(
    gdf_nodes_snapped,
    gdf_rail_stops_grouped,
    node_mask=gdf_nodes_snapped["FixedTransit"],
    max_distance_m=200,
    label="FixedTransit_Rail",
    target_id_cols=["stop_name", "stop_id"],
)
```

### Step 4b — Post-Snap Summary (Transit)

```{python}
transit_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "FixedTransit_Rail"
]["snap_distance_m"]
print(f"Transit nodes snapped:   {len(transit_snapped):,}")
print(f"  median move:           {transit_snapped.median():.1f}m")
print(f"  max move:              {transit_snapped.max():.1f}m")
```

---

## Overall Snap Summary

```{python}
print("Snap results by rule:")
print(gdf_nodes_snapped.value_counts("snap_rule").rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string())

print(f"\nTotal nodes:       {len(gdf_nodes_snapped):,}")
print(f"Snapped:           {gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].sum():,}  ({gdf_nodes_snapped['snapped'].mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Exceeded threshold:{( gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'exceeded_threshold').sum():,}")
print(f"Not attempted:     {(gdf_nodes_snapped['snap_rule'] == 'none').sum():,}")
```

```{python}
# Nodes that exceeded threshold — inspect to understand why
bool_cols = [c for c in gdf_nodes_snapped.columns if gdf_nodes_snapped[c].dtype == bool]
gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold"][
    ["N", "snap_distance_m"] + bool_cols
].sort_values("snap_distance_m", ascending=False)
```

### Conflation Performance Metrics

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import numpy as np

# 1. Isolate successfully snapped nodes
snapped_nodes = gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snapped"] == True]

# 2. Generate KPI Table
kpi_table = (
    snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule")["snap_distance_m"]
    .agg(
        Nodes_Snapped="count",
        Median_Dist_m="median",
        P90_Dist_m=lambda x: np.percentile(x, 90),
        Max_Dist_m="max",
    )
    .round(1)
)

print("=== SNAPPING PERFORMANCE KPIs ===")
print(kpi_table.to_string())
print(
    "\nTotal Nodes Exceeding Threshold:",
    (gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] == "exceeded_threshold").sum(),
)

# 3. Generate ECDF Plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
fig.patch.set_facecolor("white")
ax.set_facecolor("white")

# Define consistent colors for your rules
color_map = {
    "Freeway_Mainline": "#1D9E75",
    "Freeway_Junction": "#534AB7",
    "Interchange":      "#EF9F27",
    "Surface":          "#378ADD",
    "FixedTransit_Rail":"#888780",
}

# Plot a cumulative step curve for each rule
for rule, group_data in snapped_nodes.groupby("snap_rule"):
    distances = np.sort(group_data["snap_distance_m"].dropna())
    # Calculate cumulative probability
    y = np.arange(1, len(distances) + 1) / len(distances)

    ax.step(
        distances,
        y,
        where="post",
        label=f"{rule} (n={len(distances):,})",
        color=color_map.get(rule, "#000000"),
        linewidth=2,
    )

# Formatting
ax.set_xlim(0, 200)  # Zoom in on the first 200m to see micro-improvements
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.05)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.PercentFormatter(1.0))
ax.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.6)

ax.set_title("Algorithm Precision: Cumulative Snapping Distances", fontsize=14, pad=15)
ax.set_xlabel("Displacement Distance (meters)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel("Percentage of Nodes Snapped", fontsize=11)
ax.legend(loc="lower right", fontsize=10, frameon=True)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Export

```{python}
out_path = config_filepath["out_node_snapped"]
out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_nodes_snapped.to_file(out_path, driver="GPKG")
print(f"Snapped nodes saved to: {out_path}")
```
